result.stdout:
Infrascript CLI

For more information on how to use this tool, visit: https://www.infrascript.com/resources/docs

usage: infrascript [command] [client-id]

Commands:
  update                          Create or update resources in the blueprint
  destroy                         Destroy all existing resources in the blueprint
  preview                         Preview resources in the blueprint that would be created or updated

Client Id:
  [client-id] is a reference to a blueprint in your project. The client is made up of 4 parts: '{organization-identifier}/{project-identifier}/{blueprint-identifier}/v{version-number}'
  Version number is an optional part that can be used to execute a specific version, the other 3 parts are required for execution

Access Key:
  Execution requires an access key for the project generated from the Infrascript web application.
  The access key can be provided as a flag:
    --key, --access-key [key]
  Or as environment variable: INFRASCRIPT_ACCESS_KEY

Environment:
  The environment to execute the blueprint for can be specified using flag:
    --env, --environment [environment-identifier]
  If no environment is specified, the default configurations for the blueprint are used

Plugins: TODO

Backend-State:
  The storage for each blueprints state can be configured from the Infrascript web application.
  Depending on the provider selected, additional environment variabled may be required for execution.
  See https://www.infrascript.com/resources/docs for more details.
